title: botryopanax joskei
intro: Botryopanax joskei, also known as the New Zealand kawakawa, is a shrub-like plant native to New Zealand and parts of the Pacific Islands, belonging to the Araliaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Botryopanax joskei include its ability to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, including arthritis, digestive issues, and stress.
